title: erigeron chihuahuanus
intro: Erigeron chihuahuanus, also known as the Chihuahuan fleabane or Mexican daisy, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Erigeron chihuahuanus has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, infections, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its leaves can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
